title: pittosporum tenuifolium
intro: Pittosporum tenuifolium, also known as Kohuhu or New Zealand tobacco, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Southeast Asia. The leaves and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Pittosporum tenuifolium include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Pittosporum tenuifolium is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
